project.name: archie
project.description: Static-publishable, multi-media exhibit annotation platform. Users annotate deep-zoom images, audio, and video in a browser-based Studio (Svelte SPA), then publish self-contained static sites (Astro Viewer). Notes are W3C Web Annotation records, exhibits are IIIF Presentation 3 manifests.

nodes.name: Exhibit Authoring
nodes.summary: The Studio SPA domain where authors create and manage libraries, exhibits, and media objects. Provides a visual environment for importing media, arranging objects into exhibits, setting layout types (single/grid/narrative), and organizing narrative sections.

businessRules: Exhibit owns its Objects -- no shared object pool
Layout = reading-intent declaration, inferred from exhibit structure
Single source of truth: three persistence configs behind one Project seam
crossDomainInteractions: Consumes baked media from Media Processing
Initiates IIIF Publishing for static site export

nodes.name: Annotation Spine
nodes.summary: Core append-only annotation log system that stores, serializes, and merges W3C Web Annotation records. Provides CRUD operations (appendNew, appendEdit, appendDelete), WADM-compliant serialization to AnnotationPages, history deserialization, conflict detection, and head projection for multi-branch reconciliation.

businessRules: Append-only log -- never mutate in place
Multi-layer: layers = per-note membership, tags = purpose:tagging bodies
Three-tier degradation: Archie viewer filters, pure WADM shows all
crossDomainInteractions: Annotations projected into IIIF AnnotationPages for publishing
Annotations queried and filtered for Viewer consumption
Annotations created by Exhibit Authoring domain

nodes.name: IIIF Publishing
nodes.summary: Projects exhibit data into IIIF Presentation 3 manifests, collections, and exhibits listings. Generates IIIF Canvases per media object, attaches annotation pages with rights statements, resolves thumbnails, and produces the final hierarchical static site tree on a Zip filesystem for deployment.

businessRules: Annotations serialized as W3C AnnotationPages within IIIF structure
Published output is purely static -- no server required
Media assets bundled or referenced via IIIF Image API
crossDomainInteractions: Consumes annotation output from Annotation Spine
Provides published static tree for Viewer Presentation
Initiated by Exhibit Authoring publish command

nodes.name: Viewer Presentation
nodes.summary: The Astro-based static viewer that renders published exhibits for end-users. Handles hash-based routing (gallery/exhibit/deep-link), loading exhibits from hosted or portable (zip) sources, resolving layout types, mounting OpenSeadragon deep-zoom canvases, and filtering annotations by layer, tag, or reading.

businessRules: Single viewer shell owns all exhibits -- slug-qualified hash routing
Two data sources behind one API: hosted (HTTP) and portable (ZipFilesystem)
crossDomainInteractions: Reads published output from IIIF Publishing
Filters annotations via Annotation Spine query layer
Consumes media processed by Media Processing domain

nodes.name: Media Processing
nodes.summary: Handles media import and transformation including EXIF orientation normalization (baking upright display masters from phone photos) and AV transcript ingestion (converting WebVTT/SRT timed text into annotation notes with media-fragments temporal selectors).

businessRules: ZERO orientation-awareness in coordinate layer -- always bake upright master on import
Transcript import is import-only v1 -- no client-side ASR
Temporal selectors use media-fragments t=start,end grammar
crossDomainInteractions: Provides baked display masters to Exhibit Authoring for object source references
Produces annotation notes appended to Annotation Spine log

nodes.name: Manage Library
nodes.summary: Create, open, browse, and persist authoring projects. Uses OPFS for working storage, File System Access API for folder-bound projects, and IndexedDB for handle persistence across reloads.

nodes.name: Author Exhibit Structure
nodes.summary: Import media objects into an exhibit, arrange reading order, set layout type, and organize objects into narrative sections.

nodes.name: Annotate Media
nodes.summary: Select a media object on the canvas, draw annotation regions (rectangle/polygon), write note content, attach tags/layers/readings, and save to the annotation spine.

nodes.name: Record Annotation
nodes.summary: Append new, edit, or delete annotation records to the append-only log. Serialize to WADM-compliant AnnotationPages and deserialize from history pages.

nodes.name: Merge and Resolve
nodes.summary: Load concurrent annotation histories, detect conflicting edits, apply merge strategy, and rebuild head projections for multi-branch reconciliation.

nodes.name: Generate IIIF Manifest
nodes.summary: Project an exhibit into a IIIF Presentation 3 Manifest with Canvases per media object, annotation pages, rights statements, and thumbnail resolution.

nodes.name: Build IIIF Collection
nodes.summary: Gather all exhibits from a library and generate a IIIF Presentation 3 Collection referencing each exhibit's manifest.

nodes.name: Publish Static Site
nodes.summary: Serializes a Library + annotation log into a IIIF-based static site on a Zip filesystem with asset bundling, annotation body rewriting, and IIIF tree generation.

nodes.name: Export Portable Archive
nodes.summary: Create a self-contained .archie.zip archive with bundled media assets, library metadata, and annotation data for offline or portable viewing.

nodes.name: Browse Gallery
nodes.summary: Parse viewer hash route to gallery view, load the published library, render exhibit cards, and navigate to selected exhibit.

nodes.name: Read Exhibit
nodes.summary: Load a published exhibit by slug, resolve layout type, mount the deep-zoom canvas with annotations, and navigate between objects.

nodes.name: Open Portable Archive
nodes.summary: Accept an .archie.zip file, initialize an in-memory ZipFilesystem, switch viewer mode from hosted to portable, and resolve embedded media to blob URLs.

nodes.name: Ingest Image with EXIF
nodes.summary: Read EXIF orientation from a JPEG image, compute the geometric transform for upright normalization, and bake an orientation-blind display master for downstream consumption.

nodes.name: Import Transcript
nodes.summary: Parse a WebVTT or SRT transcript file, convert each timed cue into a W3C Annotation with motivation:supplementing targeting the AV media time range, and append to the annotation spine.

nodes.name: Open Project
nodes.summary: Pick a folder (FSA showDirectoryPicker) or create a new project, restore handle from IndexedDB, and initialize the working store.

nodes.name: Browse Exhibit List
nodes.summary: Load library metadata from OPFS and display the list of exhibits in the LibraryHome view.

nodes.name: Edit Exhibit Metadata
nodes.summary: Edit exhibit title, summary, slug, and rights information through the DetailsEditor and RightsEditor components.

nodes.name: Save Project
nodes.summary: Persist the working library to OPFS, download as a zip, or save to the user-picked folder via the File System Access API.

nodes.name: Add Media Objects
nodes.summary: Import new media objects into an exhibit, running EXIF orientation bake if needed to produce upright display masters.

nodes.name: Arrange Reading Order
nodes.summary: Reorder exhibit objects via drag-and-drop on the ExhibitOverview canvas to set the desired reading sequence.

nodes.name: Configure Layout
nodes.summary: Set the exhibit layout type (single/grid/narrative) which declares the reading intent -- explicit choice wins, otherwise inferred from structure.

nodes.name: Define Narrative Sections
nodes.summary: Organize objects into named narrative sections with optional prose, creating a structured reading path through the exhibit.

nodes.name: Select Canvas Object
nodes.summary: Click to focus a media object on the canvas, activating it for annotation. For grid layout, the selected object opens in the editor panel.

nodes.name: Draw Annotation Region
nodes.summary: Draw a rectangle or polygon region on the deep-zoom image using Annotorious, producing a W3C ImageAnnotation with SVG or fragment selector.

nodes.name: Write Note Content
nodes.summary: Enter annotation text and optional formatted body content through the canvas-anchored popover editor.

nodes.name: Attach Properties
nodes.summary: Assign tags (purpose:tagging bodies), layers (per-note membership), and readings to the annotation record for filtering and organization.

nodes.name: Save to Spine
nodes.summary: Append the new annotation record to the append-only annotation log via appendNew.

nodes.name: Edit or Delete Annotation
nodes.summary: Modify the body or target of an existing annotation (appendEdit) or mark an annotation as deleted (appendDelete).

nodes.name: Append New Annotation
nodes.summary: Create a new AnnotationRecord with generated ClientId, body, target, and motivation, then append to the log.

nodes.name: Append Edit
nodes.summary: Record an edit to an existing annotation by appending a new record with wasRevisionOf pointing to the prior version.

nodes.name: Append Delete
nodes.summary: Record a deletion tombstone for an annotation, marking it as removed without erasing history.

nodes.name: Serialize to WADM
nodes.summary: Convert the internal log format to W3C Web Annotation Data Model AnnotationPages and HeadsPages with prov:wasRevisionOf provenance chains.

nodes.name: Deserialize from WADM
nodes.summary: Read WADM-formatted history annotation pages back into the internal append-only log format for reconstruction.

nodes.name: Load Concurrent Histories
nodes.summary: Load multiple log branches or history pages from different sessions for conflict detection.

nodes.name: Detect Conflicts
nodes.summary: Compare concurrent log entries to identify conflicting edits on the same annotation record.

nodes.name: Apply Merge Strategy
nodes.summary: Resolve conflicting edits using the merge strategy rules to produce a unified history.

nodes.name: Rebuild Heads
nodes.summary: Regenerate head projections from the merged history, producing the current-state view of all annotations.

nodes.name: Build IIIF Canvases
nodes.summary: Create IIIF Canvas resources for each media object in the exhibit, with dimensions, thumbnail, and image service references.

nodes.name: Add Annotation Pages
nodes.summary: Attach W3C AnnotationPages to each Canvas, serializing the annotation spine records into the IIIF structure.

nodes.name: Attach Rights Statements
nodes.summary: Add requiredStatement, rights logo, and attribution information to the IIIF manifest per the exhibit's rights configuration.

nodes.name: Resolve Thumbnails
nodes.summary: Resolve and attach thumbnails for each Canvas from the IIIF Image API or inline media reference.

nodes.name: Collect Exhibits
nodes.summary: Gather all exhibits from the Library structure to create an exhibit listing.

nodes.name: Generate Collection Manifest
nodes.summary: Build the IIIF Presentation 3 Collection wrapper that references each exhibit's manifest.

nodes.name: Link Manifests
nodes.summary: Reference each exhibit's Manifest URL within the items array of the Collection.

nodes.name: Collect Files
nodes.summary: Gather all media assets, annotation records, and library metadata from the working store for publication.

nodes.name: Build IIIF Static Tree
nodes.summary: Generate the complete IIIF static site hierarchy: manifests, exhibits.json, annotation pages, and asset references.

nodes.name: Rewrite Annotation Bodies
nodes.summary: Rewrite annotation body content for static deployment, including Archie link resolution and media URL rewriting.

nodes.name: Write to Filesystem
nodes.summary: Write the complete published site tree to the output Zip filesystem or disk location.

nodes.name: Create Zip Filesystem
nodes.summary: Initialize an in-memory ZipFilesystem to hold the portable archive contents.

nodes.name: Bundle Media Assets
nodes.summary: Copy all media assets into the portable zip archive so it is self-contained.

nodes.name: Write Library Metadata
nodes.summary: Save the publishable library.json and exhibit metadata into the portable archive.

nodes.name: Produce Archive Bytes
nodes.summary: Serialize the ZipFilesystem to bytes and trigger download of .archie.zip file.

nodes.name: Parse Gallery Route
nodes.summary: Parse the empty/base hash route (#/) to determine the gallery view should be shown.

nodes.name: Load Library
nodes.summary: Fetch the published library data from the hosted static tree or portable zip.

nodes.name: Render Gallery
nodes.summary: Display all exhibits as cards with thumbnails, titles, and summaries in the gallery view.

nodes.name: Select Exhibit
nodes.summary: Navigate to an exhibit view by updating the hash route to #/<slug>.

nodes.name: Load Exhibit
nodes.summary: Fetch the exhibit manifest and annotations from the published data source using the exhibit slug.

nodes.name: Resolve Layout
nodes.summary: Determine the exhibit layout (single/grid/narrative) from explicit setting or structure inference.

nodes.name: Render Canvas
nodes.summary: Mount the OpenSeadragon deep-zoom viewer for the current media object, handling tile source initialization.

nodes.name: Display Annotations
nodes.summary: Load and render annotation shapes on the canvas via Annotorious, filtered by layer, tag, or reading.

nodes.name: Navigate Objects
nodes.summary: Move between exhibit objects within the layout, updating the canvas and annotation display.

nodes.name: Receive Zip File
nodes.summary: Accept an .archie.zip file via file input or drag-and-drop in the viewer.

nodes.name: Initialize ZipFilesystem
nodes.summary: Load the zip file into an in-memory ZipFilesystem using fflate for decompression.

nodes.name: Switch Viewer Mode
nodes.summary: Toggle the viewer from hosted mode to portable mode, changing the read path from HTTP to in-memory filesystem.

nodes.name: Resolve Media URLs
nodes.summary: Create blob URLs for embedded media assets in the portable archive so the OSD viewer and media players can consume them.

nodes.name: Read EXIF Orientation
nodes.summary: Scan the JPEG file's APP1/Exif segment for the Orientation tag (0x0112) and return value 1-8.

nodes.name: Compute Orientation Transform
nodes.summary: Map the EXIF Orientation tag (1-8) to the geometric transform needed for upright normalization and compute normalized dimensions.

nodes.name: Bake Display Master
nodes.summary: Use createImageBitmap and canvas to re-encode the image as an upright orientation-blind display master that everything downstream consumes.

nodes.name: Parse Transcript
nodes.summary: Parse WebVTT or SRT format into structured cue objects with start/end timestamps and text content.

nodes.name: Convert Cues to Notes
nodes.summary: Transform each transcript cue into a W3C Annotation with motivation:supplementing targeting the media time range via FragmentSelector.

nodes.name: Append Notes to Spine
nodes.summary: Append all generated annotation records to the annotation spine log via appendNew.

edges.description: Studio records annotations via spine appendNew/appendEdit/appendDelete
Studio consumes baked display masters and imported transcript annotations
Studio initiates publish of authored exhibits into IIIF static sites
Annotation spine records serialized into IIIF AnnotationPages for published manifests
Viewer reads published IIIF static tree (manifests, annotation pages, assets)
Viewer filters annotations by layer, tag, and reading via query/filter layer
Transcript import produces annotation records appended to the spine
